top_concerns.issue: Belgium name value is likely too high relative to two straight low-conversion matches.
Egypt second-half pressure and Salah finishing route may still be underweighted, but Iran draw and set-piece tail must remain live.
Same-time third-round scoreboard dependence means motivation cannot be modeled as static for 90 minutes.
China Sports Lottery market snapshot is missing or not open, so market calibration must be downweighted.
No round-3 quant output exists yet, so this review cannot be presented as full quant-backed validation.

upset_or_failure_scenarios: Belgium controls territory again but fails to convert, pushing the match toward a low-score draw tail.
New Zealand starts more conservative than expected, then opens late because a win is required.
Egypt shifts into draw-management mode in the second half and reduces its own win rate.
Iran scores first via set piece or counter and breaks the single-side Egypt favorite story.
Real-time score changes in the other match alter late-game incentives.
overconfidence_flags: Revise if Belgium win probability is above 0.56.
Revise if Egypt win probability is above 0.41 while draw is below 0.30.
Revise if any match is framed with a narrow score band despite incomplete upstream inputs.
market_flags: Missing or unopened China Sports Lottery prices cannot be treated as stable support.
If only generic odds are available and no executable lottery line exists, keep discussion_only.
quant_chain_flags: data-collector.json is still missing standings, odds snapshot, and source crosscheck.
tactics-coach.json still lacks official T-75 lineups, discipline confirmation, and medical confirmation.
modeler-draft.json is still a partial skeleton.

belgium_vs_new_zealand.direction: Trim Belgium favorite inflation and raise draw plus late-open New Zealand tail.

egypt_vs_iran.direction: Keep a small Egypt edge, but preserve a thick draw band and Iran set-piece/counter tail.

handoff_notes: data: add standings, qualification paths, same-time motivation notes, and market snapshot.
tactics: recheck Belgium finishing and rest-defense issues, New Zealand pace split, Egypt second-half pressure, and Iran set-piece/counter routes.
model: cap Belgium favorite premium, avoid double counting Egypt tactical lift plus star finishing, and preserve Iran draw/upset tail.
main: publish only as partial discussion output, not as full quant consensus.
